title: LaTeX editing, compilation, and language-intelligence stack
summary: VimTeX owns LaTeX project structure, latexmk compilation, quickfix parsing, mappings, and PDF synchronization; texlab independently owns language-server intelligence and explicit formatting.
details: lua/custom/plugins/vimtex.lua loads lervag/vimtex eagerly at the plugin-manager level as required for VimtexInverseSearch, while VimTeX itself defers substantive work through filetype and autoload mechanisms.

VimTeX uses the installed latexmk backend in continuous mode only after an explicit compile action. It parses compiler results and owns table-of-contents, cleanup, project-main-file, and viewer integration.

Viewer selection is executable- and platform-aware. Okular receives --unique and SyncTeX source-position arguments on the current host; no repository code edits the viewer's external inverse-search preference.
nvim-lspconfig configures texlab for tex, plaintex, and bib buffers. Mason tool installer owns the texlab executable; project root markers fall back to a standalone file's directory.
Texlab's build-on-save and forward-search-after-build paths are disabled to prevent overlap with VimTeX. ChkTeX runs only on open/save when the executable exists, not on every edit.
latexindent is exposed through explicit LSP/Conform formatting with line-break modification disabled. Conform returns no save-time format request for Markdown, TeX, plain TeX, or BibTeX.
The markup FileType/BufWinEnter gate is separate from both tools and enforces literal-source window options even when a window was previously used by the concealing C++ frontend.
Tree-sitter highlighting and indentation are disabled for tex, plaintex, and bib so VimTeX remains the sole syntax/indent owner and no redundant LaTeX parser is auto-installed.
rationale: VimTeX handles multi-file TeX compilation, log parsing, main-document discovery, and SyncTeX more completely than rebuilding those workflows locally.
Texlab adds structural language intelligence without needing to become a second compiler owner.
Explicit build and format actions avoid surprising process launches and large document rewrites on an ordinary save.

provenance.note: Owner requested LaTeX support after establishing literal-source Markdown presentation.

The host already provided latexmk, pdflatex, latexindent, chktex, biber, SyncTeX, MuPDF, and Okular; texlab and editor integration were absent. Official VimTeX and texlab interfaces were verified before implementation.

evidence.claim: Plugin commands, buffer mappings, literal-source settings, texlab configuration, root-independent setup, and no-build-on-save behavior are pinned in isolated Neovim.
Compilation/viewing and language intelligence have separate explicit owners and no save-triggered build path.

questions: Which component compiles LaTeX and which one provides completion?
Why is VimTeX not lazy-loaded through lazy.nvim?
How is the PDF viewer selected?
Why does texlab not build or format on save?

last_verified.method: Host executable inventory, pinned official upstream documentation, installed-plugin inspection, and focused headless contract.
